title: Exelixis — White Space
html:
Exelixis White-Space BD Map

Last updated: 2026-05-05 Purpose: Identify, prioritize, and plan pursuit of untapped revenue across Exelixis brands x ZoomRx services. The central BD thesis: Exelixis is transitioning from cabozantinib to zanzalintinib, and every ZoomRx opportunity revolves around supporting this pipeline transition.

How to Read This Matrix

Legend:

  • Active: Currently contracted or in-flight
  • HIGH: Pursue now — strong fit, clear entry path, or active RFP/proposal
  • MEDIUM: Pursue FY26-27 — good fit but requires contact development or timing alignment
  • LOW: Long-term or speculative — no current entry path
  • N/A: Not applicable or poor fit

Scoring rationale:

  • HIGH is reserved for services where (a) an RFP or proposal already exists, (b) the service naturally follows from active work, or (c) the service is critical to the zanzalintinib launch and ZoomRx has demonstrated capability
  • MEDIUM indicates good strategic fit but a dependency on contact development, timing, or winning a prerequisite engagement
  • LOW indicates a viable service but no near-term entry path

Oncology: CABOMETYX + Zanzalintinib

Service CABOMETYX (cabozantinib) Zanzalintinib (pre-launch) Zanzalintinib (launch/post-launch)
PET / SFEA Active (2019, stale) HIGH HIGH
Integrated Tracking Active (2023, completed) MEDIUM MEDIUM
ATU MEDIUM HIGH (RFP in hand) HIGH
Unbranded Campaign Impact N/A Active N/A
Branded Campaign Testing LOW HIGH (proposal submitted) HIGH
HCP-Patient (HCPP) MEDIUM MEDIUM HIGH
MSL / MEE MEDIUM HIGH HIGH
OCE LOW MEDIUM MEDIUM
FRM LOW MEDIUM MEDIUM
KAM LOW LOW LOW
Digital Tracking LOW MEDIUM HIGH
Launch KPI Tracking N/A HIGH HIGH
Perxcept Active (2024, completed) MEDIUM MEDIUM
Patient Experience / Scribe LOW MEDIUM MEDIUM
Message Labs / AI Dynamic Dialogues LOW HIGH MEDIUM
Qual Research LOW MEDIUM MEDIUM
Commercial Analytics / SFTO LOW LOW MEDIUM
Sales Simulation LOW MEDIUM LOW
Ferma Congress LOW LOW LOW
Social Listening LOW MEDIUM MEDIUM

Matrix Summary

Priority Count Notes
Active 3 cells Campaign Impact (active), PET (2019/stale), Integrated Tracking (2023/completed), Perxcept (2024/completed)
HIGH 8 cells Concentrated in zanzalintinib pre-launch and launch: ATU (RFP), Branded Campaign Testing (proposal), PET, MSL/MEE, Launch KPI, Message Labs
MEDIUM ~16 cells Broad mid-term pipeline dependent on contact development and launch timing
LOW ~14 cells Long-term or poor fit for current account state

Top Opportunities (Ranked)

Rank Brand / Phase Service Priority Est. Revenue Entry Strategy Timeline Rationale
1 Zanzalintinib (pre-launch) ATU HIGH $50-100K/yr RFP in hand. Respond with integrated insights pitch — connect ATU to active campaign impact data. Differentiate on oncology expertise and AI-enhanced analytics. FY26 H1 (decision imminent) RFP exists. Winning creates 2-3 year tracking relationship and opens door for every other service. This is the #1 priority on the account.
2 Zanzalintinib (pre-launch) Branded Campaign Testing HIGH $50-100K Proposal submitted (v0.2). Iterative dialogue signals engagement. Push to close. Frame as natural next step from unbranded campaign impact: "You shaped the market; now optimize your branded messages." FY26 H1 (proposal active) Proposal exists. Winning creates upstream input to PET. Combined with ATU and campaign impact, creates three-legged pre-launch platform.
3 Zanzalintinib (launch) PET (Launch Tracking) HIGH $100-200K/yr Propose after ATU or campaign testing win. Pitch: "You tested the messages (campaign testing); you tracked market awareness (ATU); now track how well your field force delivers those messages (PET)." Reference 2019 cabo PET as proof of oncology PET capability. FY26 H2 - FY27 H1 ZoomRx's heritage service at Exelixis. Natural downstream from campaign testing. Multi-wave annual tracking = highest per-service revenue.
4 Zanzalintinib (pre-launch/launch) Launch KPI Tracking HIGH $75-150K Propose during launch planning phase (6-12 months before expected approval). Position as the comprehensive launch readiness and performance dashboard. FY26 H2 - FY27 Standard launch research. Exelixis will need this — the question is vendor selection. Being embedded via ATU/campaign testing makes ZoomRx the natural choice.
5 Zanzalintinib (pre-launch/launch) MSL / MEE Tracking HIGH $75-150K/yr Requires Medical Affairs contact development. Oncology MSLs are critical for TKI differentiation — scientific exchange drives prescribing in RCC and HCC. Pitch: "MSLs are your scientific voice; are they delivering the right data at the right time?" FY26 H2 - FY27 Oncology MSL teams are large and influential. MSL/MEE tracking is a high-value recurring service. No current vendor relationship to displace (that we know of).
6 Zanzalintinib (pre-launch) Message Labs / AI Dynamic Dialogues HIGH $50-100K Propose alongside or after branded campaign testing. ZoomRx's AI-powered message testing methodology adds a layer of sophistication beyond traditional campaign testing. FY26 H1-H2 Perxcept adoption (2024) proves Exelixis is open to ZoomRx proprietary methods. Message Labs extends this to AI-powered testing.
7 CABOMETYX ATU (lifecycle) MEDIUM $50-75K/yr Propose as a parallel workstream to zanzalintinib ATU. CABOMETYX is still a $2B+ brand — lifecycle management requires ongoing market tracking, especially as zanzalintinib cannibalizes share. FY26-27 Dual-brand ATU (cabo + zanza) creates a franchise-level view that Exelixis cannot get from a vendor running only one brand's ATU.
8 Zanzalintinib (launch/post-launch) HCP-Patient Conversations (HCPP) MEDIUM-HIGH $50-75K Propose after PET or ATU establishes the tracking relationship. Frame HCPP as the qualitative complement: "ATU tells you what physicians think; HCPP tells you what they actually say to patients in the exam room." FY27 HCPP is ZoomRx's wedge product at other accounts (Pfizer, J&J). At Exelixis, the entry path is through quantitative work first (ATU, PET), then add HCPP for depth.

Revenue Summary of Top 8

Metric Estimate
Total potential (all 8 at full scope) $500K - $950K annually
Realistic close rate (40-50% across all 8) $200K - $475K
Near-term pipeline (ATU + Branded Campaign Testing only) $100K - $200K
Launch-phase pipeline (PET + Launch KPI + MSL) $250K - $500K

Summary

The Exelixis white-space story is simple and compelling: a one-franchise oncology company is about to launch its most important product, and ZoomRx is positioned at the front door with active work and live proposals.

Three things must happen to convert white space into revenue:

  1. Win the ATU and branded campaign testing bids. These are the gateway. Every other opportunity (PET, Launch KPI, MSL, HCPP) flows downstream from establishing ZoomRx as a trusted zanzalintinib research partner. Losing these bids doesn't just lose $100-200K — it loses the platform.

  2. Build the integrated insights narrative. ZoomRx's differentiation is not any single service — it is the ability to connect campaign impact + ATU + message testing + PET into a unified pre-launch and launch intelligence platform. No vendor starting from zero can match this. Every proposal should articulate this vision.

  3. Expand the contact map into Medical Affairs and Field Operations. The current relationships (whoever commissioned campaign impact and issued the ATU RFP) likely sit in the Market Research or Brand Strategy function. MSL/MEE, PET, and OCE opportunities require relationships with Medical Affairs and Sales/Field Operations leaders. This is the medium-term BD work that unlocks the second wave of services.

The cabo-to-zanzalintinib transition is a once-in-a-franchise event. The research vendor decisions being made now will define multi-year relationships. The account team should treat the next 6 months as a window — not an ongoing opportunity.

This map should be refreshed when ATU and branded campaign testing decisions are made, or when zanzalintinib clinical milestones are announced. Next refresh: upon RFP outcome or August 2026, whichever is sooner.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
